items.hook: ‘Cool, suave and weirdly shy’: Miranda Richardson remembers Sam Neill
items.body: Sam had such ease about him and I just loved the way he seemed to cruise through life. But he confronted his mortality with real courage

items.hook: King Leopold III of Belgium abdicates in favor of his son, Baudouin of Belgium.
items.body: Leopold III was King of the Belgians from 23 February 1934 until his abdication on 16 July 1951. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: J. D. Salinger publishes his popular yet controversial novel, The Catcher in the Rye.
items.body: Jerome David Salinger was an American author best known for his 1951 novel The Catcher in the Rye. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: KLM Flight 844 crashes off the Schouten Islands in present day Indonesia (then Netherlands New Guinea), killing 58 people.
items.body: KLM Flight 844 was an international scheduled passenger flight from Biak-Mokmer Airport, Dutch New Guinea to Manila International Airport, Manila, Philippines on 16 July 1957, which crashed into Cenderawasih Bay 1.2 kilometres from its departure airport. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Odyssey is a 'colossal piece of cinema', critics say in rave reviews
items.body: The film, starring Matt Damon and Zendaya, is director Sir Christopher Nolan's first since Oppenheimer.

items.hook: American Civil War: At the order of President Abraham Lincoln, Union troops begin a 25-mile march into Virginia for what will become the First Battle of Bull Run, the first major land battle of the war.
items.body: The American Civil War was a civil war in the United States between the Union and the Confederacy, which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Yoel Cohen is a British-born Israeli scholar of mass communication and Professor Emeritus at Ariel University, where he previously served as the head of the School of Communication.
items.body: His research focuses on media diplomacy, political communication, and religion and media, with particular attention to journalism systems and state–media relations. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Chaplain–Medic massacre: American POWs are massacred by North Korean Army.
items.body: The Chaplain–Medic massacre took place in the Korean War on July 16, 1950, on a mountain above the village of Tuman. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Friendly Hills, also known as Margaret Culkin Banning House, is a historic estate located near Tryon, Polk County, North Carolina.
items.body: The house was built in 1924, and is a large, two-story, Tudor Revival style dwelling with stuccoed exterior walls decorated with hewn, half timbering. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Odyssey review – Nolan goes god-tier with breathtaking epic of men, monsters and moral metamorphosis
items.body: Doing full justice to the Homeric legend, Christopher Nolan amasses an epic cast to convey the true cost of war with film-making of thrilling ambition

items.hook: Edward Arthur Fellowes Prynne (1854–1921) was a leading British painter of portraits and subject pictures, who in later life became one of the country's best-known creators of decorative art for churches.
items.body: Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Zhao Kuangning (趙匡凝), courtesy name Guangyi (光儀), formally the Prince of Chu (楚王), was a warlord late in the Chinese Tang dynasty, who ruled Zhongyi Circuit as its military governor (jiedushi) from 892 until his defeat in 905 by Zhu Quanzhong the military governor of Xuanwu Circuit.
items.body: One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Claudia Winkleman quits chat show after one series
items.body: Winkleman said she was "incredibly grateful" for the opportunity, but had been "too nervous" to enjoy it.

items.hook: The Ivy Council is a non-profit organization of Ivy League student government leaders, student organization leaders, and students at-large.
items.body: It was established in 1990 by members of the Ivy League student governments to facilitate communication among themselves and to speak with a unified voice. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Rickie, Melvin and Dean McCullough to leave Radio 1 in schedule shake-up
items.body: Jeremiah Asiamah will join Charlie Hedges in the Live Lounge, among other changes.

items.hook: A classicist’s verdict on Nolan’s Odyssey: a soulful hero flatters our times as women and nuance pushed overboard
items.body: Matt Damon’s sensitive and repentant Odysseus might come as a surprise to Homer, likewise some significant omissions concerning the poem’s female characters
England's defeat to Argentina in the World Cup semi-final attracts an audience of 24 million on the BBC.

items.hook: Father Junípero Serra founds California's first mission, Mission San Diego de Alcalá. Over the following decades, it evolves into the city of San Diego, California.
items.body: Junípero Serra Ferrer, popularly known simply as Junipero Serra, was a Spanish Catholic priest and missionary of the Franciscan Order. The world is stranger than it lets on.
